name: Maranhao (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the sky over Maranhao; field of the canton

description: Nine equal horizontal stripes of red, white, black, white, red, white, black, white and red, with a blue canton in the upper hoist bearing a single white five-pointed star.
history: Designed by the poet Sousandrade and adopted by Decree No. 6 of 21 December 1889, shortly after the Proclamation of the Republic; the colours represent the three peoples that make up Maranhao's population, and the star stands for Beta Scorpii in the constellation Scorpius.

features.symbolism: Nine equal horizontal stripes alternating red, white and black; the colours represent the multiracial composition of Maranhao's people
Blue canton bearing a single white star
Beta Scorpii, the star representing Maranhao on the celestial sphere of the Brazilian national flag
